sourceMovie.title: Infernal Affairs
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Infernal Affairs (2002) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Police Story 4: First Strike
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Infernal Affairs clicked for you, Police Story 4: First Strike (1996) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and action, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Raging Fire
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Infernal Affairs clicked for you, Raging Fire (2021) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Infernal Affairs?
faq.answer: Try Police Story 4: First Strike, Raging Fire, SPL 2: A Time for Consequences—each shares DNA with Infernal Affairs while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Infernal Affairs?
faq.answer: Infernal Affairs (2002) is best described as Drama, Action, Thriller, Crime, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Infernal Affairs part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Infernal Affairs Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
